en.extract:
The greater green leafbird (Chloropsis sonnerati) is a species of bird in the family Chloropseidae. It is distinguished from the lesser green leafbird (Chloropsis cyanopogon) by its powerful beak, yellow throat and eye ring of the female; and lack of a yellow border along the black throat patch found in the male C. cyanopogan.
It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, Singapore, and Thailand. In Indonesia, it is found in Sumatra, Borneo, Natuna Islands, Java and Bali.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical mangrove forest, mainly old-growth forest but also secondary forest and edges.
It moves quite conspicuously at the canopy level, jumping between branches and flying from tree to tree. It often visits fruiting fig trees, but also takes insects and small invertebrates.
The greater green leafbird has a loud voice, consisting of an ascending whistle chee-zi-chee.
The species is threatened by trapping for the cage bird trade and has become rare or even disappeared in large parts of its range.


References

strange, Morten (2000). A Photographic Guide to the Birds of Thailand Including Southeast Asia & The Philippines (1st ed.). Eric Oey (Periplus). ISBN 962-593-926-1.


External links
Greater Green Leafbird videos on the Internet Bird Collection
Photos on the Oriental Bird Club
Male and female illustration on Hong Kong Park - Specialties
Voice recordings on xeno-canto Asia

[[File:Greater Green Leafbird (Imm).jpg|thumb|Greater Green Leafbird (Immature)]]
The '''greater green leafbird''' ('''''Chloropsis sonnerati''''') is a [[species]] of [[bird]] in the family [[Chloropseidae]]. It is distinguished from the [[lesser green leafbird]] (''Chloropsis cyanopogon'') by its powerful [[Bird anatomy|beak]], yellow throat and eye ring of the female; and lack of a yellow border along the black throat patch found in the male ''C. cyanopogan''.

It is found in [[Brunei]], [[Indonesia]], [[Malaysia]], [[Myanmar]], [[Singapore]], and [[Thailand]]. In Indonesia, it is found in Sumatra, Borneo, Natuna Islands, Java and Bali.
Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical [[mangrove forest]], mainly [[old-growth forest]] but also [[secondary forest]] and edges.

It moves quite conspicuously at the [[Canopy (biology)|canopy]] level, jumping between branches and flying from tree to tree. It often visits fruiting [[ficus|fig trees]], but also takes insects and small invertebrates.

The greater green leafbird has a loud voice, consisting of an ascending whistle ''chee-zi-chee''.

The species is threatened by trapping for the [[Cage-bird trade|cage bird trade]] and has become rare or even disappeared in large parts of its range.
